statement.en: Chitinase-3-like 1 is associated with stunting and developmental delay in HIV exposed but uninfected Ugandan children
statement.summary: Chronic systemic inflammation may affect linear growth and neurodevelopment in children who are HIV exposed but uninfected (cHEU). We examined plasma concentrations of neutrophil activation marker chitinase-3-like protein 1 (CHI3L1) levels in 153 Ugandan cHEU. At 18 months of age, CHI3L1 levels were inversely correlated with height-for-age z-scores (τB = -0.17, p=0.0035) and a normalized developmental score (τB = -0.20, p=0.00027). CHI3L1 appears to be a marker of adverse growth and development in cHEU.

proposer: Elspeth MacBain, Andrea L. Conroy, J Brophy et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The study presents empirical findings from a prospective cohort (n=153) with statistically significant correlations (p<0.01), but the work is dated 2026-04-25 (future date, indicating preprint/provisional status). The causal mechanism linking CHI3L1 to stunting/developmental delay remains mechanistically underdetermined. Findings are consistent with inflammation-mediated developmental pathways but require independent replication and mechanistic validation before elevation to established fact. Classification as FLOWING reflects provisional empirical support awaiting peer review and external validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 炎症の火が消えるほど、子どもの身体は空へ伸びていく。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 炎症の靄が晴れるとき、成長の根は天へ向かう光を見つける。
